name: New Jersey Data Privacy Act
summary: The New Jersey Data Privacy Act grants New Jersey consumers rights over their personal data and imposes obligations on controllers and processors. It applies to entities conducting business in New Jersey or targeting its residents that process data of at least 100,000 consumers, or 25,000 consumers if deriving revenue from selling personal data.
keyRequirements: Right to access personal data collected by a controller
Right to correct inaccuracies in personal data
Right to delete personal data provided by or obtained about the consumer
Right to obtain a copy of personal data in a portable format
Right to opt out of the sale of personal data, targeted advertising, and profiling
Requirement to conduct data protection assessments for high-risk processing activities
Prohibition on processing sensitive data without consumer consent
penalties: Up to $10,000 per violation for first offenses; up to $20,000 per violation for subsequent offenses. Enforced exclusively by the New Jersey Attorney General; no private right of action.
